_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement).

title: Technical Report–Study/Services
summary: This Data Item Description specifies format and content instructions for technical reports, ensuring comprehensive documentation of studies or analyses performed, as required by contract.
use_relationship: A technical report provides fully documented results of studies or analysis performed.
description_purpose: This Data Item Description (DID) contains format and content preparation instructions for the data product generated by the specific and discrete task requirement as delineated in the contract.
distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

children.title: The report and all attachments shall be typewritten, or otherwise clearly lettered, and shall be duplicated using non-fading ink.
Text shall be prepared on standard letter size paper (8 1/2 x 11").
When attachments are included, they shall be fully identified, referenced in the text, and folded to conform to the size paper used in the report.
Security classification and distribution markings shall conform to the requirements of the contract, purchase description and security requirements checklist, as applicable.
Title Page – Identifies the report by providing contract number, project name or purchase description title, task number, and reporting period.
Summary - A brief statement of results obtained for the analytic effort.
Conclusions and their condensed technical substantiation's.
Section II – A complete and detailed description of the analytic results which led to the conclusions stated in Section I above.
